 `./board/planning/NOTE-0029_server-side-rendering-for-crawlers.md`

```text
1	---
2	type: note
3	status: inbox
4	created: 2026-04-06
5	---
6	
7	# Server-side rendering for crawlers and LLMs
8	
9	All page content is currently rendered client-side, so crawlers and LLMs see an empty shell. We need SSR/SSG for the home page (the only page that exists).
10	
11	## Recommended approach
12	
13	[`vite-react-ssg`](https://vite-react-ssg.netlify.app/docs/Getting-Started) — light lift, stays within the existing Vite + React stack. Pre-renders the home page at build time so the HTML is crawlable without JS execution.
14	
15	## Current setup
16	
17	- Single-page app: `main.tsx` uses `createRoot` (client-only rendering)
18	- No router — `App.tsx` renders one page directly
19	- Vite config is minimal: `@vitejs/plugin-react` + `@tailwindcss/vite`
20	- Framer Motion `MotionConfig` wraps the app at the root level
21	
22	## Migration considerations
23	
24	- `vite-react-ssg` replaces `createRoot` with its own entry point; the main change is in `main.tsx`
25	- Single-route app so routing setup is trivial (one route: `/`)
26	- `usePalette` reads from Zustand store and computes colors — palette generation is deterministic from defaults, so SSG can capture the initial render
27	- `useDynamicFavicon` manipulates `<link>` tags in the DOM — needs to be guarded or deferred to client-only execution
28	- Framer Motion works with SSR but animations will hydrate on the client
29	- Charts (canvas-based via `ChartTabs`) may need lazy/client-only rendering if they depend on `window` or `document`
30	
31	## What crawlers will see
32	
33	After SSG, the pre-rendered HTML will include the full default palette grid, swatch values, header, and control labels. Interactive state changes (hue/saturation adjustments) remain client-side, which is fine — crawlers get the meaningful content.
34	
[result-id: r4]
```
</details></tool-use>

You are reviewing a React app's source code to validate that existing board/task items for adding SSG (static site generation) are complete and cover all implementation details.

## Context

The project is adding SSG via `vite-react-ssg` to pre-render the home page so crawlers see real HTML instead of an empty shell. The app is at `/Users/grant/code/chroma-curve/app/`.

## Board tasks to validate against

**TASK-0047**: Install vite-react-ssg and configure Vite plugin
**TASK-0048**: Migrate main.tsx — replace `createRoot` with vite-react-ssg entry, define single route `/`, preserve StrictMode and MotionConfig wrappers
**TASK-0049**: Audit browser-only code — guard `document`, `navigator`, `Image`, `URL.createObjectURL` usage. Key files listed: useDynamicFavicon.ts, useCopyToClipboard.ts, export.ts, TargetSwatch.tsx
**TASK-0050**: Validate build output — confirm index.html has pre-rendered content, not empty div#root

## Your job

1. Read `main.tsx` and `App.tsx` to understand the current entry point and root component structure. Document exactly what TASK-0048 needs to change.

2. Read `vite.config.ts` to understand the current plugin setup. Document what TASK-0047 needs to change.

3. Read ALL files listed in TASK-0049 (useDynamicFavicon.ts, useCopyToClipboard.ts, export.ts, TargetSwatch.tsx) and also scan for ANY OTHER files that use browser-only APIs (`document`, `window`, `navigator`, `Image`, `canvas`, `URL.createObjectURL`, `localStorage`, `sessionStorage`, `matchMedia`, `requestAnimationFrame`, `IntersectionObserver`, `ResizeObserver`, `MutationObserver`). Report which files are safe and which need guards, and whether TASK-0049's file list is complete.

4. Check `index.html` to see the current shell structure.

5. Check `package.json` for any scripts that may need updating (dev, build, preview).

6. Look at any components that use Framer Motion — do they have SSR-incompatible patterns?

7. Check if there are any dynamic imports, lazy loading, or code splitting that could affect SSG.

**Output a structured report** with:
- Section per task (0047–0050) listing what's accurate, what's missing, and what's wrong
- A "Missing tasks" section for anything not covered by existing tasks
- A "Risk areas" section for things that could go wrong during implementation

Be thorough. Read every file that could be relevant. This is a research-only task — do not edit any files.
</teammate-message>

1	import { useEffect, useRef } from "react";
2	import { usePaletteStore } from "../store/paletteStore";
3	
4	const SIZE = 32;
5	
6	function buildFaviconSvg(hue: number): string {
7	  return `<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 ${SIZE} ${SIZE}">
8	  <defs>
9	    <linearGradient id="sat" x1="0" y1="0" x2="1" y2="0">
10	      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="white"/>
11	      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="hsl(${hue}, 100%, 50%)"/>
12	    </linearGradient>
13	    <linearGradient id="val" x1="0" y1="0" x2="0" y2="1">
14	      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="black" stop-opacity="0"/>
15	      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="black"/>
16	    </linearGradient>
17	    <clipPath id="clip"><rect width="${SIZE}" height="${SIZE}"/></clipPath>
18	    <filter id="shadow"><feDropShadow dx="0" dy="0" stdDeviation="1.5" flood-color="black" flood-opacity="0.5"/></filter>
19	  </defs>
20	  <g clip-path="url(#clip)">
21	    <rect width="${SIZE}" height="${SIZE}" fill="url(#sat)"/>
22	    <rect width="${SIZE}" height="${SIZE}" fill="url(#val)"/>
23	  </g>
24	  <rect width="${SIZE}" height="${SIZE}" fill="none" stroke="hsl(0,0%,20%)" stroke-width="1.5"/>
25	  <path d="M24 2 Q-2 16 24 30" fill="none" stroke="white" stroke-width="2.5" stroke-linecap="round" filter="url(#shadow)"/>
26	</svg>`;
27	}
28	
29	function renderSvgToPngHref(svg: string): Promise<string> {
30	  return new Promise((resolve) => {
31	    const img = new Image();
32	    const blob = new Blob([svg], { type: "image/svg+xml;charset=utf-8" });
33	    const url = URL.createObjectURL(blob);
34	
35	    img.onload = () => {
36	      const canvas = document.createElement("canvas");
37	      canvas.width = SIZE;
38	      canvas.height = SIZE;
39	      const ctx = canvas.getContext("2d")!;
40	      ctx.drawImage(img, 0, 0, SIZE, SIZE);
41	      URL.revokeObjectURL(url);
42	      resolve(canvas.toDataURL("image/png"));
43	    };
44	
45	    img.src = url;
46	  });
47	}
48	
49	function setFavicon(href: string) {
50	  // Remove all existing icon links so browsers don't prefer a static fallback
51	  document
52	    .querySelectorAll<HTMLLinkElement>('link[rel="icon"]')
53	    .forEach((el) => el.remove());
54	
55	  const link = document.createElement("link");
56	  link.rel = "icon";
57	  link.type = "image/png";
58	  link.href = href;
59	  document.head.appendChild(link);
60	}
61	
62	export function useDynamicFavicon() {
63	  const hue = usePaletteStore((s) => s.hue);
64	  const pending = useRef(0);
65	
66	  useEffect(() => {
67	    const id = ++pending.current;
68	    const svg = buildFaviconSvg(hue);
69	
70	    renderSvgToPngHref(svg).then((href) => {
71	      // Drop stale renders if hue changed while we were rasterizing
72	      if (id !== pending.current) return;
73	      setFavicon(href);
74	    });
75	  }, [hue]);
76	}
77

1	import { useState, useCallback, useRef } from "react";
2	
3	export function useCopyToClipboard(timeout = 1500) {
4	  const [copiedLabel, setCopiedLabel] = useState<string | null>(null);
5	  const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null);
6	  const timerRef = useRef<ReturnType<typeof setTimeout>>(undefined);
7	
8	  const copy = useCallback(
9	    (text: string, label: string) => {
10	      navigator.clipboard
11	        .writeText(text)
12	        .then(() => {
13	          setCopiedLabel(label);
14	          setError(null);
15	          clearTimeout(timerRef.current);
16	          timerRef.current = setTimeout(() => setCopiedLabel(null), timeout);
17	        })
18	        .catch((err: unknown) => {
19	          setCopiedLabel(null);
20	          setError(err instanceof Error ? err.message : "Copy failed");
21	          console.warn("Clipboard write failed:", err);
22	        });
23	    },
24	    [timeout],
25	  );
26	
27	  return { copiedLabel, error, copy };
28	}
29

1	import chroma from "chroma-js";
2	import type { ColorEntry, ExportFormat, ColorSpace } from "../types/palette";
3	
4	function colorValue(entry: ColorEntry, space: ColorSpace): string {
5	  const c = chroma(entry.hex);
6	  switch (space) {
7	    case "hsl":
8	      return `hsl(${entry.hslString})`;
9	    case "hex":
10	      return entry.hex;
11	    case "rgb": {
12	      const [r, g, b] = c.rgb();
13	      return `rgb(${r}, ${g}, ${b})`;
14	    }
15	    case "hcl": {
16	      const [h, c2, l] = c.hcl();
17	      const hR = Math.round(h || 0);
18	      const cR = Math.round((c2 || 0) * 10) / 10;
19	      const lR = Math.round(l * 10) / 10;
20	      return `hcl(${hR}, ${cR}, ${lR})`;
21	    }
22	    case "oklch": {
23	      const [l, c2, h] = c.oklch();
24	      const lR = Math.round(l * 1000) / 1000;
25	      const cR = Math.round((c2 || 0) * 1000) / 1000;
26	      const hR = Math.round(h || 0);
27	      return `oklch(${lR} ${cR} ${hR})`;
28	    }
29	  }
30	}
31	
32	export function formatPalette(
33	  entries: ColorEntry[],
34	  format: ExportFormat,
35	  space: ColorSpace,
36	): string {
37	  switch (format) {
38	    case "css":
39	      return formatCSS(entries, space);
40	    case "tw4":
41	      return formatTailwindV4(entries, space);
42	    case "tw3":
43	      return formatTailwindV3(entries, space);
44	    case "scss":
45	      return formatSCSS(entries, space);
46	    case "js":
47	      return formatJS(entries, space);
48	    case "json":
49	      return formatJSON(entries, space);
50	    case "gpl":
51	      return formatGPL(entries);
52	    case "text":
53	      return formatText(entries, space);
54	  }
55	}
56	
57	function formatCSS(entries: ColorEntry[], space: ColorSpace): string {
58	  const vars = entries
59	    .map((e) => `  --${e.label}: ${colorValue(e, space)};`)
60	    .join("\n");
61	  return `:root {\n${vars}\n}`;
62	}
63	
64	function formatTailwindV4(entries: ColorEntry[], space: ColorSpace): string {
65	  const vars = entries
66	    .map((e) => `  --color-${e.label}: ${colorValue(e, space)};`)
67	    .join("\n");
68	  return `@theme {\n${vars}\n}`;
69	}
70	
71	function formatTailwindV3(entries: ColorEntry[], space: ColorSpace): string {
72	  const lines: string[] = [];
73	  for (const e of entries) {
74	    lines.push(`        ${String(e.lightness)}: "${colorValue(e, space)}",`);
75	  }
76	  return [
77	    "/** @type {import('tailwindcss').Config} */",
78	    "module.exports = {",
79	    "  theme: {",
80	    "    extend: {",
81	    "      colors: {",
82	    '        grey: {',
83	    ...lines,
84	    "        },",
85	    "      },",
86	    "    },",
87	    "  },",
88	    "};",
89	  ].join("\n");
90	}
91	
92	function formatSCSS(entries: ColorEntry[], space: ColorSpace): string {
93	  return entries
94	    .map((e) => `$${e.label}: ${colorValue(e, space)};`)
95	    .join("\n");
96	}
97	
98	function formatJS(entries: ColorEntry[], space: ColorSpace): string {
99	  const colors: Record<string, string> = {};
100	  for (const e of entries) {
101	    colors[String(e.lightness)] = colorValue(e, space);
102	  }
103	  return `export const grey = ${JSON.stringify(colors, null, 2)} as const;`;
104	}
105	
106	function formatJSON(entries: ColorEntry[], space: ColorSpace): string {
107	  const tokens: Record<string, { $value: string; $type: string }> = {};
108	  for (const e of entries) {
109	    tokens[String(e.lightness)] = {
110	      $value: colorValue(e, space),
111	      $type: "color",
112	    };
113	  }
114	  return JSON.stringify({ grey: tokens }, null, 2);
115	}
116	
117	function formatGPL(entries: ColorEntry[]): string {
118	  const lines = ["GIMP Palette", "Name: Grey Neutrals", `Columns: ${entries.length}`, "#"];
119	  for (const e of entries) {
120	    const c = chroma(e.hex);
121	    const [r, g, b] = c.rgb();
122	    lines.push(`${String(Math.round(r)).padStart(3)} ${String(Math.round(g)).padStart(3)} ${String(Math.round(b)).padStart(3)}\t${e.label}`);
123	  }
124	  return lines.join("\n");
125	}
126	
127	function formatText(entries: ColorEntry[], space: ColorSpace): string {
128	  const maxLabel = Math.max(...entries.map((e) => e.label.length));
129	  return entries
130	    .map((e) => `${e.label.padEnd(maxLabel)}  ${colorValue(e, space)}`)
131	    .join("\n");
132	}
133	
134	const FORMAT_EXTENSIONS: Record<ExportFormat, string> = {
135	  css: "css",
136	  tw4: "css",
137	  tw3: "js",
138	  scss: "scss",
139	  js: "ts",
140	  json: "json",
141	  gpl: "gpl",
142	  text: "txt",
143	};
144	
145	const FORMAT_MIME_TYPES: Record<ExportFormat, string> = {
146	  css: "text/css",
147	  tw4: "text/css",
148	  tw3: "text/javascript",
149	  scss: "text/css",
150	  js: "text/javascript",
151	  json: "application/json",
152	  gpl: "text/plain",
153	  text: "text/plain",
154	};
155	
156	export function downloadPalette(
157	  entries: ColorEntry[],
158	  format: ExportFormat,
159	  space: ColorSpace,
160	): void {
161	  const content = formatPalette(entries, format, space);
162	  const ext = FORMAT_EXTENSIONS[format];
163	  const mime = FORMAT_MIME_TYPES[format];
164	  const blob = new Blob([content], { type: `${mime};charset=utf-8` });
165	  const url = URL.createObjectURL(blob);
166	  const a = document.createElement("a");
167	  a.href = url;
168	  a.download = `palette.${ext}`;
169	  a.click();
170	  URL.revokeObjectURL(url);
171	}
172

You are researching the `vite-react-ssg` library to validate a planned SSG integration for a React + Vite app.

## Context

The project at `/Users/grant/code/chroma-curve/app/` is a single-page React 19 app using:
- Vite with `@vitejs/plugin-react` and `@tailwindcss/vite`

The plan is to use `vite-react-ssg` to pre-render the home page at build time.

- Whether the existing board tasks accurately reflect the library's actual API

This is research only — do not edit any files.

1	import { useCallback, useEffect, useMemo, useRef, useState } from "react";
2	import chroma from "chroma-js";

6	import type { ColorFormat } from "../../lib/colors";
7	
8	const PICKER_SIZE = 200;
9	
10	function ColorInput({ currentHex, sourceInput, sourceFormat, hue, saturation, onApply }: {
11	  currentHex: string;
12	  sourceInput: string | null;
13	  sourceFormat: ColorFormat | null;
14	  hue: number;
15	  saturation: number;
16	  onApply: (hue: number, saturation: number, input: string, format: ColorFormat) => void;
17	}) {
18	  const [value, setValue] = useState("");
19	  const [isInvalid, setIsInvalid] = useState(false);
20	
21	  const targetFormatted = sourceFormat
22	    ? formatTargetColor(hue, saturation, sourceFormat)
23	    : currentHex;
24	
25	  const handleKeyDown = (e: React.KeyboardEvent<HTMLInputElement>) => {
26	    if (e.key === "Enter") {
27	      const result = parseTargetColor(value);
28	      if (result) {
29	        onApply(result.hue, result.saturation, value.trim(), result.format);
30	        setValue("");
31	        setIsInvalid(false);
32	      } else {
33	        setIsInvalid(true);
34	      }
35	    } else if (e.key === "Escape") {
36	      setValue("");
37	      setIsInvalid(false);
38	      (e.target as HTMLInputElement).blur();
39	    }
40	  };
41	
42	  return (
43	    <div className="mt-2">
44	      <div className="mb-1.5 space-y-0.5">
45	        {sourceInput ? (
46	          <>
47	            <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
48	              <span className="text-3xs text-text-faint uppercase tracking-wider">Input</span>
49	              <span className="text-xs font-mono text-text-faint">{sourceInput}</span>
50	            </div>
51	            <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
52	              <span className="text-3xs text-text-faint uppercase tracking-wider">H+S at L50</span>
53	              <span className="text-xs font-mono text-text-secondary">{targetFormatted}</span>
54	            </div>
55	          </>
56	        ) : (
57	          <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
58	            <span className="text-3xs text-text-faint uppercase tracking-wider">Target</span>
59	            <span className="text-xs font-mono text-text-secondary">{currentHex}</span>
60	          </div>
61	        )}
62	      </div>
63	      <input
64	        type="text"
65	        value={value}
66	        onChange={(e) => { setValue(e.target.value); setIsInvalid(false); }}
67	        onKeyDown={handleKeyDown}
68	        placeholder="#hex, rgb(), hsl(), oklch()"
69	        className={`w-full bg-surface-base border px-2 py-1.5 text-xs font-mono text-text-primary placeholder:text-text-faint outline-none transition-colors ${isInvalid ? "border-red-500" : "border-border-default focus:border-text-faint"}`}
70	      />
71	    </div>
72	  );
73	}

75	export function TargetSwatch() {
76	  const hue = usePaletteStore((s) => s.hue);
77	  const saturation = usePaletteStore((s) => s.saturation);
78	  const setHue = usePaletteStore((s) => s.setHue);
79	  const setSaturation = usePaletteStore((s) => s.setSaturation);
80	  const canvasRef = useRef<HTMLCanvasElement>(null);
81	  const draggingRef = useRef(false);
82	  const [sourceInput, setSourceInput] = useState<string | null>(null);
83	  const [sourceFormat, setSourceFormat] = useState<ColorFormat | null>(null);
84	  const [sourceHue, setSourceHue] = useState<number | null>(null);
85	  const [sourceSat, setSourceSat] = useState<number | null>(null);
86	
87	  // Clear source when hue/sat diverge from what the input set
88	  const effectiveSourceInput = (
89	    sourceInput && sourceHue === hue && sourceSat === saturation
90	  ) ? sourceInput : null;
91	  const effectiveSourceFormat = effectiveSourceInput ? sourceFormat : null;
92	
93	  const color = `hsl(${hue}, ${saturation * 100}%, 50%)`;
94	  const currentHex = useMemo(() => chroma.hsl(hue, saturation, 0.5).hex(), [hue, saturation]);
95	
96	  const drawPicker = useCallback(
97	    (canvas: HTMLCanvasElement) => {
98	      const ctx = canvas.getContext("2d");
99	      if (!ctx) return;
100	      const w = PICKER_SIZE;
101	      const h = PICKER_SIZE;
102	
103	      // Draw hue (x) vs saturation (y) at L=50%
104	      const imageData = ctx.createImageData(w, h);
105	      for (let y = 0; y < h; y++) {
106	        for (let x = 0; x < w; x++) {
107	          const h360 = (x / (w - 1)) * 360;
108	          const s = 1 - y / (h - 1);
109	          // HSL to RGB conversion
110	          const c = (1 - Math.abs(2 * 0.5 - 1)) * s;
111	          const hp = h360 / 60;
112	          const x2 = c * (1 - Math.abs((hp % 2) - 1));
113	          let r1 = 0,
114	            g1 = 0,
115	            b1 = 0;
116	          if (hp < 1) {
117	            r1 = c;
118	            g1 = x2;
119	          } else if (hp < 2) {
120	            r1 = x2;
121	            g1 = c;
122	          } else if (hp < 3) {
123	            g1 = c;
124	            b1 = x2;
125	          } else if (hp < 4) {
126	            g1 = x2;
127	            b1 = c;
128	          } else if (hp < 5) {
129	            r1 = x2;
130	            b1 = c;
131	          } else {
132	            r1 = c;
133	            b1 = x2;
134	          }
135	          const m = 0.5 - c / 2;
136	          const i = (y * w + x) * 4;
137	          imageData.data[i] = Math.round((r1 + m) * 255);
138	          imageData.data[i + 1] = Math.round((g1 + m) * 255);
139	          imageData.data[i + 2] = Math.round((b1 + m) * 255);
140	          imageData.data[i + 3] = 255;
141	        }
142	      }
143	      ctx.putImageData(imageData, 0, 0);
144	
145	      // Draw crosshair at current hue/sat position
146	      const cx = (hue / 360) * (w - 1);
147	      const cy = (1 - saturation) * (h - 1);
148	      ctx.strokeStyle = "white";
149	      ctx.lineWidth = 1.5;
150	      ctx.beginPath();
151	      ctx.arc(cx, cy, 5, 0, Math.PI * 2);
152	      ctx.stroke();
153	      ctx.strokeStyle = "rgba(0,0,0,0.4)";
154	      ctx.lineWidth = 1;
155	      ctx.beginPath();
156	      ctx.arc(cx, cy, 6, 0, Math.PI * 2);
157	      ctx.stroke();
158	    },
159	    [hue, saturation],
160	  );
161	
162	  const handleCanvasRef = useCallback(
163	    (canvas: HTMLCanvasElement | null) => {
164	      canvasRef.current = canvas;
165	      if (canvas) drawPicker(canvas);
166	    },
167	    [drawPicker],
168	  );
169	
170	  const pickFromCoords = useCallback(
171	    (clientX: number, clientY: number) => {
172	      const canvas = canvasRef.current;
173	      if (!canvas) return;
174	      const rect = canvas.getBoundingClientRect();
175	      if (
176	        clientX < rect.left ||
177	        clientX > rect.right ||
178	        clientY < rect.top ||
179	        clientY > rect.bottom
180	      ) return;
181	      const x = (clientX - rect.left) / rect.width;
182	      const y = (clientY - rect.top) / rect.height;
183	      setHue(Math.round(x * 360));
184	      setSaturation(parseFloat((1 - y).toFixed(2)));
185	      setSourceInput(null);
186	      setSourceFormat(null);
187	    },
188	    [setHue, setSaturation],
189	  );
190	
191	  const handleMouseDown = useCallback(
192	    (e: React.MouseEvent<HTMLCanvasElement>) => {
193	      e.preventDefault();
194	      draggingRef.current = true;
195	      pickFromCoords(e.clientX, e.clientY);
196	    },
197	    [pickFromCoords],
198	  );
199	
200	  useEffect(() => {
201	    const handleMouseMove = (e: MouseEvent) => {
202	      if (draggingRef.current) pickFromCoords(e.clientX, e.clientY);
203	    };
204	    const handleMouseUp = () => {
205	      draggingRef.current = false;
206	    };
207	    document.addEventListener("mousemove", handleMouseMove);
208	    document.addEventListener("mouseup", handleMouseUp);
209	    return () => {
210	      document.removeEventListener("mousemove", handleMouseMove);
211	      document.removeEventListener("mouseup", handleMouseUp);
212	    };
213	  }, [pickFromCoords]);
214	
215	  return (
216	    <Popover.Root>
217	      <Popover.Trigger asChild>
218	        <button
219	          type="button"
220	          className="flex flex-col items-center gap-1 shrink-0 self-end cursor-pointer group"
221	          aria-label={`Target color: ${currentHex}`}
222	        >
223	          <div
224	            className="w-7 h-7 rounded-full border-2 border-surface-base transition-transform group-hover:scale-110 shadow-[0_0_0_1px_rgba(255,255,255,0.15)]"
225	            style={{ backgroundColor: color }}
226	          />
227	          <span className="text-3xs font-mono text-text-faint group-hover:text-text-faint transition-colors">{currentHex}</span>
228	        </button>
229	      </Popover.Trigger>
230	      <Popover.Portal>
231	        <Popover.Content
232	          sideOffset={8}
233	          className="rounded-none bg-surface-overlay border border-border-elevated shadow-xl p-3 z-[60]"
234	        >
235	          <canvas
236	            ref={handleCanvasRef}
237	            width={PICKER_SIZE}
238	            height={PICKER_SIZE}
239	            className="cursor-crosshair block"
240	            style={{ width: PICKER_SIZE, height: PICKER_SIZE }}
241	            onMouseDown={handleMouseDown}
242	          />
243	          <div className="flex justify-between mt-2 text-sm text-text-faint">
244	            <span>Hue {hue}°</span>
245	            <span>Sat {Math.round(saturation * 100)}%</span>
246	          </div>
247	          <ColorInput
248	            currentHex={currentHex}
249	            sourceInput={effectiveSourceInput}
250	            sourceFormat={effectiveSourceFormat}
251	            hue={hue}
252	            saturation={saturation}
253	            onApply={(h, s, input, format) => { setHue(h); setSaturation(s); setSourceInput(input); setSourceFormat(format); setSourceHue(h); setSourceSat(s); }}
254	          />
255	          <Popover.Arrow className="fill-surface-overlay" />
256	        </Popover.Content>
257	      </Popover.Portal>
258	    </Popover.Root>
259	  );
260	}
261

Based on the search results, here's information about the vite-react-ssg library:

## Overview

vite-react-ssg provides static-site generation for React on Vite. The official GitHub repository is at https://github.com/Daydreamer-riri/vite-react-ssg.

## Key API Components

### Route Configuration
The library uses `RouteRecord[]` from 'vite-react-ssg' to define routes, supporting lazy loading and `getStaticPaths` to determine which paths will be pre-rendered.

### Development vs Production
vite-react-ssg provides SSR (Server-Side Rendering) during development to ensure consistency between development and production, but you can use CSR during development by configuring your package.json scripts.

### Utility Components

**ClientOnly Component**: The `ClientOnly` component from 'vite-react-ssg' allows wrapping client-only code, with children as a function that returns an element, since React will try to render children and may use the client's API on the server.

**Head Component**: The `<Head/>` component manages changes to the document head, takes plain HTML tags and outputs plain HTML tags, and is a wrapper around React Helmet.

### Data Fetching
You can use react-router-dom's loader to fetch data at build time and use useLoaderData to get the data in the component; in production, the loader only executes at build time, and data is fetched by the manifest generated at build time during browser navigations.

## React Router Version Support

React Router v7 now has built-in SSG support, and if you are using React Router v7, built-in SSG capabilities are recommended, though vite-react-ssg will continue to maintain SSG functionality for React Router v6 users.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

You are analyzing the build pipeline, deployment, and hydration concerns for adding SSG to a React + Vite app.

## Context

The project at `/Users/grant/code/chroma-curve/app/` is adding SSG via `vite-react-ssg` to pre-render the home page. The app is deployed (check for any deployment config — Cloudflare Pages, Vercel, Netlify, etc.).

## Board tasks being validated

- TASK-0047: Install vite-react-ssg, configure Vite plugin
- TASK-0048: Migrate main.tsx entry point
- TASK-0049: Guard browser-only code
- TASK-0050: Validate build output HTML

## Your job

1. Read `vite.config.ts` to understand the full build configuration.

2. Read `package.json` for build scripts, dependencies, and any deployment-related config.

3. Check for deployment configuration files: `wrangler.toml`, `wrangler.jsonc`, `vercel.json`, `netlify.toml`, `.github/workflows/`, or any CI/CD config in the project root `/Users/grant/code/chroma-curve/` and the app directory.

4. Read `index.html` to understand the current HTML shell — what's in `<head>`, meta tags, fonts, scripts.

5. Check `tsconfig.json` and `tsconfig.app.json` for any SSR-incompatible TypeScript config (e.g., DOM lib assumptions, module resolution).

6. Look at Tailwind config — does the build pipeline need changes for SSG to generate CSS correctly?

7. Analyze these concerns:
   - **Hydration mismatch risk**: Are there components that render differently on server vs client? (e.g., date/time, random values, window-size-dependent rendering)
   - **Font loading**: The app uses Adobe Fonts/Typekit — will fonts be available during SSG build?
   - **Meta tags / SEO**: Does the current index.html have proper meta tags, or does SSG need to inject them?
   - **Asset paths**: Will static asset references work correctly in SSG output?
   - **CSS generation**: Will Tailwind CSS be properly included in the SSG output?
   - **Build script changes**: What npm scripts need updating?
   - **Deployment impact**: Will the deployment pipeline need changes?

**Output a structured report** with:
- Current build pipeline analysis
- Deployment configuration findings
- Hydration risk assessment
- Missing concerns not covered by existing tasks (TASK-0047 through TASK-0050)
- Recommended additional tasks or modifications to existing tasks
- Implementation order recommendations

This is research only — do not edit any files.

1	import { useMemo } from "react";
2	import { usePaletteStore } from "../store/paletteStore";
3	import { generateNeutrals } from "../lib/palette";
4	import type { ColorEntry } from "../types/palette";
5	
6	export function usePalette(): ColorEntry[] {
7	  const hue = usePaletteStore((s) => s.hue);
8	  const saturation = usePaletteStore((s) => s.saturation);
9	  const sMod = usePaletteStore((s) => s.sMod);
10	  const satMode = usePaletteStore((s) => s.satMode);
11	  const lMin = usePaletteStore((s) => s.lMin);
12	  const lMax = usePaletteStore((s) => s.lMax);
13	
14	  return useMemo(
15	    () => generateNeutrals({ hue, saturation, sMod, satMode, lMin, lMax }),
16	    [hue, saturation, sMod, satMode, lMin, lMax],
17	  );
18	}
19

1	import { useMemo, useState } from "react";
2	import * as Tabs from "@radix-ui/react-tabs";
3	import chroma from "chroma-js";
4	import { Triangle } from "@phosphor-icons/react";
5	import { SaturationCurve } from "./SaturationCurve";
6	import { EffectiveSaturation } from "./EffectiveSaturation";
7	import { ToggleSwitch } from "../controls/ToggleSwitch";
8	import { usePaletteStore } from "../../store/paletteStore";
9	import type { ColorEntry } from "../../types/palette";
10	
11	interface ChartTabsProps {
12	  entries: ColorEntry[];
13	}
14	
15	export function ChartTabs({ entries }: ChartTabsProps) {
16	  const [showDots, setShowDots] = useState(true);
17	  const [showCompare, setShowCompare] = useState(false);
18	  const hue = usePaletteStore((s) => s.hue);
19	  const saturation = usePaletteStore((s) => s.saturation);
20	
21	  const { unadjustedColors, adjustedColors } = useMemo(() => ({
22	    unadjustedColors: entries.map((e) =>
23	      chroma.hsl(hue, saturation, e.lightness / 100).hex(),
24	    ),
25	    adjustedColors: entries.map((e) => e.hex),
26	  }), [entries, hue, saturation]);
27	
28	  return (
29	    <div className="mx-4 md:mx-0 rounded-none border border-border-default bg-surface-raised overflow-hidden lg:mr-4">
30	      <div className="px-4 py-2.5 flex items-center justify-between md:hidden lg:flex">
31	        <h2 className="text-sm font-semibold text-center w-full uppercase text-text-muted tracking-wider">
32	          Saturation Curve
33	        </h2>
34	      </div>
35	      <Tabs.Root defaultValue="adjustment">
36	        <Tabs.List className="flex border-y border-border-default">
37	          <Tabs.Trigger
38	            value="adjustment"
39	            className="flex-1 px-4 py-2 md:px-2 md:py-2 lg:px-4 lg:py-2 text-xs font-medium uppercase tracking-wider md:tracking-normal lg:tracking-wider text-text-faint cursor-pointer transition-colors data-[state=active]:text-text-primary border-r border-border-default"
40	          >
41	            Adjustment
42	          </Tabs.Trigger>
43	          <Tabs.Trigger
44	            value="computed"
45	            className="flex-1 px-4 py-2 text-xs font-medium uppercase tracking-wider md:tracking-normal lg:tracking-wider text-text-faint cursor-pointer transition-colors data-[state=active]:text-text-primary"
46	          >
47	            Computed
48	          </Tabs.Trigger>
49	        </Tabs.List>
50	        <Tabs.Content value="adjustment" forceMount className="data-[state=inactive]:hidden">
51	          <h3 className="sr-only">Adjustment <span>multiplier</span></h3>
52	          <SaturationCurve entries={entries} showDots={showDots} />
53	        </Tabs.Content>
54	        <Tabs.Content value="computed" forceMount className="data-[state=inactive]:hidden">
55	          <h3 className="sr-only">Computed <span>values</span></h3>
56	          <EffectiveSaturation entries={entries} showDots={showDots} />
57	        </Tabs.Content>
58	      </Tabs.Root>
59	      <div className="px-4 py-2 flex items-center justify-between md:hidden lg:flex">
60	        <button
61	          type="button"
62	          onClick={() => setShowCompare(!showCompare)}
63	          className="text-sm text-text-faint font-semibold cursor-pointer select-none hover:text-text-muted transition-colors md:hidden lg:inline-flex lg:items-center lg:gap-1"
64	        >
65	          <Triangle size={7} weight="fill" className={`transition-transform ${showCompare ? "rotate-180" : "rotate-90"}`} /> Compare
66	        </button>
67	        <ToggleSwitch label="steps" checked={showDots} onChange={setShowDots} />
68	      </div>
69	      {showCompare && (
70	        <div className="px-4 pb-3 space-y-2 md:hidden lg:block">
71	          <div>
72	            <span className="text-xs text-text-faint block mb-1">Unadjusted</span>
73	            <div className="h-6 rounded-none overflow-hidden grid grid-flow-col auto-cols-fr">
74	              {unadjustedColors.map((c, i) => (
75	                <div key={i} style={{ backgroundColor: c }} />
76	              ))}
77	            </div>
78	          </div>
79	          <div>
80	            <span className="text-xs text-text-faint block mb-1">Adjusted</span>
81	            <div className="h-6 rounded-none overflow-hidden grid grid-flow-col auto-cols-fr">
82	              {adjustedColors.map((c, i) => (
83	                <div key={i} style={{ backgroundColor: c }} />
84	              ))}
85	            </div>
86	          </div>
87	        </div>
88	      )}
89	    </div>
90	  );
91	}

# Vite React SSG
[](#vite-react-ssg)
Static-site generation for React on Vite.
See demo(also document): [docs](https://vite-react-ssg.netlify.app/)
Important
**React Router v7 Notice**
React Router v7 now has built-in SSG support. If you are using React Router v7, we recommend using its official SSG capabilities for better official support and integration.
`vite-react-ssg` will continue to maintain SSG functionality for React Router v6 users.
**🎈 Support for [`@tanstack/router`](https://tanstack.com/router/latest/docs/framework/react/overview) and [`wouter`](https://github.com/molefrog/wouter) is in progress!**
Support for the [`@tanstack/router`](https://tanstack.com/router/latest/docs/framework/react/overview) router is still experimental, and `pathname.lazy.tsx routes` are not yet supported. For usage examples, see: [`main`/examples/tanstack/src/main.tsx](https://github.com/Daydreamer-riri/vite-react-ssg/blob/main/examples/tanstack/src/main.tsx)
[![Mentioned in Vite Awesome](https://camo.githubusercontent.com/1667baa3145a7b9b2a0ae17d8c7b09b4d471f79aa02b0615ab07d64d9988a198/68747470733a2f2f617765736f6d652e72652f6d656e74696f6e65642d62616467652e737667)](https://github.com/vitejs/awesome-vite) [![NPM version](https://camo.githubusercontent.com/ccf03224ad05b6388a3b36c32a8068f920b55acfc7f498791ba03ad5a76ba60b/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6e706d2f762f766974652d72656163742d7373673f636f6c6f723d613162383538266c6162656c3d)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/vite-react-ssg)

## Usage
[](#usage)
**npm i -D vite-react-ssg** _react-router-dom_
```diff
// package.json
{
  "scripts": {
-   "build": "vite build"
+   "build": "vite-react-ssg build"
    // If you need ssr when dev
-   "dev": "vite",
+   "dev": "vite-react-ssg dev",
    // OR if you want to use another vite config file
+   "build": "vite-react-ssg build -c another-vite.config.ts"
  }
}
```
```ts
// src/main.ts
import { ViteReactSSG } from 'vite-react-ssg'
import routes from './App.tsx'
export const createRoot = ViteReactSSG(
  // react-router-dom data routes
  { routes },
  // function to have custom setups
  ({ router, routes, isClient, initialState }) => {
    // do something.
  },
)
```
```tsx
// src/App.tsx
import type { RouteRecord } from 'vite-react-ssg'
import React from 'react'
import Layout from './Layout'
import './App.css'
export const routes: RouteRecord[] = [
  {
    path: '/',
    element: <Layout />,
    entry: 'src/Layout.tsx',
    children: [
      {
        path: 'a',
        lazy: () => import('./pages/a'),
      },
      {
        index: true,
        Component: React.lazy(() => import('./pages/index')),
      },
      {
        path: 'nest/:b',
        lazy: () => {
          const Component = await import('./pages/nest/[b]')
          return { Component }
        },
        // To determine which paths will be pre-rendered
        getStaticPaths: () => ['nest/b1', 'nest/b2'],
      },
    ],
  },
]
```
### Use CSR during development
[](#use-csr-during-development)
Vite React SSG provide SSR (Server-Side Rendering) during development to ensure consistency between development and production as much as possible.
But if you want to use CSR during development, just:
```diff
// package.json
{
  "scripts": {
-   "dev": "vite-react-ssg dev",
+   "dev": "vite",
    "build": "vite-react-ssg build"
  }
}
```
### Single Page SSG
[](#single-page-ssg)
For SSG of an index page only (i.e. without `react-router-dom`); import `vite-react-ssg/single-page` instead.
```tsx
// src/main.tsx
import { ViteReactSSG } from 'vite-react-ssg/single-page'
import App from './App.tsx'
export const createRoot = ViteReactSSG(<App />)
```
## Extra route options
[](#extra-route-options)
The RouteObject of vite-react-ssg is based on react-router, and vite-react-ssg receives some additional properties.
#### `getStaticPaths`
[](#getstaticpaths)
The `getStaticPaths()` function should return an array of path to determine which paths will be pre-rendered by vite-react-ssg.
This function is only valid for dynamic route.
```tsx
const route = {
  path: 'nest/:b',
  lazy: () => import('./pages/nest/[b]'),
  entry: 'src/pages/nest/[b].tsx',
  // To determine which paths will be pre-rendered
  getStaticPaths: () => ['nest/b1', 'nest/b2'],
}
```
#### `entry`
[](#entry)
**You are not required to use this field. It is only necessary when "prehydration style loss" occurs.** It should be the path from root to the target file.
eg: `src/pages/page1.tsx`
## lazy
[](#lazy)
These options work well with the `lazy` field.
```tsx
// src/pages/[page].tsx
export function Component() {
  return (
    <div>{/* your component */}</div>
  )
}
export function getStaticPaths() {
  return ['page1', 'page2']
}
```
```ts
// src/routes.ts
const routes = [
  {
    path: '/:page',
    lazy: () => import('./pages/[page]'),
  }
]
```
**Note that** during the build process, `vite-react-ssg` will [automatically detect](https://github.com/Daydreamer-riri/vite-react-ssg/blob/main/src/node/assets.ts#L5) the files directly dynamically imported in the function you pass to the `lazy` field. This helps `vite-react-ssg` to get the route's style files or other static resources during the build, preventing [flash of unstyled content](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Flash_of_unstyled_content).
If you still encounter FOUC (flash of unstyled content), please open an issue.
If your component isn't loading, make sure you have wrapped it or its parent in `Suspense` tags as described in the [React documentation](https://react.dev/reference/react/lazy#usage).
See [example](https://github.com/Daydreamer-riri/vite-react-ssg/blob/main/examples/lazy-pages/src/App.tsx).
## Data fetch
[](#data-fetch)
You can use react-router-dom's `loader` to fetch data at build time and use `useLoaderData` to get the data in the component.
In production, the `loader` will only be executed at build time, and the data will be fetched by the manifest generated at build time during the browser navigations .
In the development environment, the `loader` also runs only on the server.It provides data to the HTML during initial server rendering, and during browser route navigations , it makes calls to the server by initiating a fetch on the service.
```tsx
import { useLoaderData } from 'react-router-dom'
export default function Docs() {
  const data = useLoaderData() as Awaited<ReturnType<typeof loader>>
  return (
    <>
      <div>{data.key}</div>
      {/* eslint-disable-next-line react-dom/no-dangerously-set-innerhtml */}
      <div dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{ __html: data.packageCodeHtml }} style={{ textAlign: 'start' }}></div>
    </>
  )
}
export const Component = Docs
export const entry = 'src/pages/json.tsx'
export async function loader() {
  // This code will avoid `shiki` and `node:fs` being mark as 'modulepreload' and sent to the client.
  if (!import.meta.ssr) {
    return null
  }
  // The code here will not be executed on the client side, and the modules imported will not be sent to the client.
  const fs = (await import('node:fs'))
  const cwd = process.cwd()
  const json = (await import('../docs/test.json')).default
  const packageJson = await fs.promises.readFile(`${cwd}/package.json`, 'utf-8')
  const { codeToHtml } = await import('shiki')
  const packageJsonHtml = await codeToHtml(packageJson, { lang: 'json', theme: 'vitesse-light' })
  return {
    ...json,
    packageCodeHtml: packageJsonHtml,
  }
}
```
See [example | with-loader](https://github.com/Daydreamer-riri/vite-react-ssg/blob/main/examples/with-loader/src/pages/%5Bdocs%5D.tsx).
## `<ClientOnly/>`
[](#clientonly)
If you need to render some component in browser only, you can wrap your component with `<ClientOnly>`.
```tsx
import { ClientOnly } from 'vite-react-ssg'
function MyComponent() {
  return (
    <ClientOnly>
      {() => {
        return <div>{window.location.href}</div>
      }}
    </ClientOnly>
  )
}
```
> It's important that the children of `<ClientOnly>` is not a JSX element, but a function that returns an element. Because React will try to render children, and may use the client's API on the server.
## Document head
[](#document-head)
You can use `<Head/>` to manage all of your changes to the document head. It takes plain HTML tags and outputs plain HTML tags. It is a wrapper around [React Helmet](https://github.com/nfl/react-helmet).
```tsx
import { Head } from 'vite-react-ssg'
function MyHead() {
  return (
    <Head>
      <meta property="og:description" content="My custom description" />
      <meta charSet="utf-8" />
      <title>My Title</title>
      <link rel="canonical" href="http://mysite.com/example" />
    </Head>
  )
}
```
Nested or latter components will override duplicate usages:
```tsx
import { Head } from 'vite-react-ssg'
function MyHead() {
  return (
    <parent>
      <Head>
        <title>My Title</title>
        <meta name="description" content="Helmet application" />
      </Head>
      <child>
        <Head>
          <title>Nested Title</title>
          <meta name="description" content="Nested component" />
        </Head>
      </child>
    </parent>
  )
}
```
Outputs:
```html
<head>
  <title>Nested Title</title>
  <meta name="description" content="Nested component" />
</head>
```
### Reactive head
[](#reactive-head)
```tsx
import { useState } from 'react'
import { Head } from 'vite-react-ssg'
export default function MyHead() {
  const [state, setState] = useState(false)
  return (
    <Head>
      <meta charSet="UTF-8" />
      <link rel="icon" type="image/svg+xml" href="/vite.svg" />
      <title>head test {state ? 'A' : 'B'}</title>
      {/* You can also set the 'body' attributes here */}
      <body className={`body-class-in-head-${state ? 'a' : 'b'}`} />
    </Head>
  )
}
```
## Redirect
[](#redirect)
You should not use redirect in the loader. In vite-react-ssg, the loader only executes during the build process for data fetching. If you need to perform a redirect in certain situations, you can use the following method to redirect on the client side:
```tsx
export const routes: RouteRecord[] = [
  {
    path: '/:lng',
    Component: Layout,
    getStaticPaths: () => Object.keys(resources),
    children: [
      // ... some routes
    ],
  },
  {
    path: '/',
    Component: () => {
      const navigate = useNavigate()
      useEffect(() => {
        navigate('/en', { replace: true })
      }, [navigate])
      return null
    },
  },
]
```
## Public Base Path
[](#public-base-path)
Just set `base` in vite.config.ts like:
```ts
import react from '@vitejs/plugin-react-swc'
// vite.config.ts
import { defineConfig } from 'vite'
// https://vitejs.dev/config/
export default defineConfig({
  plugins: [react()],
  base: '/base-path',
})
```
```ts
// main.ts
import { ViteReactSSG } from 'vite-react-ssg'
import { routes } from './App'
import './index.css'
export const createRoot = ViteReactSSG(
  {
    routes,
    // pass your BASE_URL
    basename: import.meta.env.BASE_URL,
  },
)
```
Vite React SSG will give it to the react-router's `basename`.
See: [react-router's create-browser-router](https://reactrouter.com/en/main/routers/create-browser-router#basename)
[Example](https://github.com/Daydreamer-riri/vite-react-ssg/blob/main/examples/lazy-pages/vite.config.ts)
## Future config
[](#future-config)
```tsx
export const createRoot = ViteReactSSG(
  {
    routes,
    basename: import.meta.env.BASE_URL,
    future: {
      v7_normalizeFormMethod: true,
      v7_startTransition: true,
      v7_fetcherPersist: true,
      v7_relativeSplatPath: true,
      v7_skipActionErrorRevalidation: true,
      v7_partialHydration: true,
    },
  },
)
```
See: [react-router's optsfuture](https://reactrouter.com/6.28.0/routers/create-browser-router#optsfuture)
[Example](https://github.com/Daydreamer-riri/vite-react-ssg/blob/main/examples/lazy-pages/src/main.tsx)
## Custom Router Factory
[](#custom-router-factory)
You can provide a custom router factory function via `customCreateRouter` option. This is useful if you want to use a different router implementation or wrap the default `createBrowserRouter`.
```ts
import { createBrowserRouter } from 'react-router-dom'
export const createRoot = ViteReactSSG(
  {
    routes,
    customCreateRouter: (routes, options) => {
      // Custom logic here
      return createBrowserRouter(routes, options)
    },
  },
)
```
eg. [sentry](https://docs.sentry.io/platforms/javascript/guides/react/features/react-router/v6/)
```ts
import * as Sentry from '@sentry/react'
import { createBrowserRouter } from 'react-router-dom'
export const createRoot = ViteReactSSG(
  {
    routes,
    customCreateRouter: Sentry.wrapCreateBrowserRouterV6(createBrowserRouter),
  },
)
```
## CSS in JS
[](#css-in-js)
Use the `getStyleCollector` option to specify an SSR/SSG style collector. Currently only supports `styled-components`.
```tsx
import { ViteReactSSG } from 'vite-react-ssg'
import getStyledComponentsCollector from 'vite-react-ssg/style-collectors/styled-components'
import { routes } from './App.js'
import './index.css'
export const createRoot = ViteReactSSG(
  { routes },
  () => { },
  { getStyleCollector: getStyledComponentsCollector }
)
```
You can provide your own by looking at the [implementation](https://github.com/Daydreamer-riri/vite-react-ssg/blob/main/src/style-collectors) of any of the existing collectors.
## Critical CSS
[](#critical-css)
Vite React SSG has built-in support for generating [Critical CSS](https://web.dev/extract-critical-css/) inlined in the HTML via the [`beasties`](https://github.com/danielroe/beasties) package. Install it with:
```shell
npm i -D beasties
```
Critical CSS generation will automatically be enabled for you.
To configure `beasties`, pass [its options](https://github.com/danielroe/beasties#usage) into `ssgOptions.beastiesOptions` in `vite.config.ts`:
```ts
// vite.config.ts
export default defineConfig({
  ssgOptions: {
    beastiesOptions: {
      // E.g., change the preload strategy
      preload: 'media',
      // Other options: https://github.com/danielroe/beasties#usage
    },
  },
})
```
## Configuration
[](#configuration)
You can pass options to Vite SSG in the `ssgOptions` field of your `vite.config.js`
```js
// vite.config.js
export default {
  plugins: [],
  ssgOptions: {
    script: 'async',
  },
}
```
```ts
interface ViteReactSSGOptions {
  /**
   * Set the scripts' loading mode. Only works for `type="module"`.
   *
   * @default 'sync'
   */
  script?: 'sync' | 'async' | 'defer' | 'async defer'
  /**
   * Build format.
   *
   * @default 'esm'
   */
  format?: 'esm' | 'cjs'
  /**
   * The path of the main entry file (relative to the project root).
   *
   * @default 'src/main.ts'
   */
  entry?: string
  /**
   * The path of the index.html file (relative to the project root).
   * @default 'index.html'
   */
  htmlEntry?: string
  /**
   * Mock browser global variables (window, document, etc...) from SSG.
   *
   * @default false
   */
  mock?: boolean
  /**
   * Apply formatter to the generated index file.
   *
   * **It will cause Hydration Failed.**
   *
   * @default 'none'
   */
  formatting?: 'prettify' | 'none'
  /**
   * Vite environment mode.
   */
  mode?: string
  /**
   * Directory style of the output directory.
   *
   * flat: `/foo` -> `/foo.html`
   * nested: `/foo` -> `/foo/index.html`
   *
   * @default 'flat'
   */
  dirStyle?: 'flat' | 'nested'
  /**
   * Generate for all routes, including dynamic routes.
   * If enabled, you will need to configure your serve
   * manually to handle dynamic routes properly.
   *
   * @default false
   */
  includeAllRoutes?: boolean
  /**
   * Options for the beasties packages.
   *
   * @see https://github.com/danielroe/beasties#usage
   */
  beastiesOptions?: BeastiesOptions | false
  /**
   * Custom function to modify the routes to do the SSG.
   *
   * Works only when `includeAllRoutes` is set to false.
   *
   * Defaults to a handler that filters out all the dynamic routes.
   * When passing your custom handler, you should also take care of the dynamic routes yourself.
   */
  includedRoutes?: (paths: string[], routes: Readonly<RouteRecord[]>) => Promise<string[]> | string[]
  /**
   * Callback to be called before every page render.
   *
   * It can be used to transform the project's `index.html` file before passing it to the renderer.
   *
   * To do so, you can change the 'index.html' file contents (passed in through the `indexHTML` parameter), and return it.
   * The returned value will then be passed to renderer.
   */
  onBeforePageRender?: (route: string, indexHTML: string, appCtx: ViteReactSSGContext<true>) => Promise<string | null | undefined> | string | null | undefined
  /**
   * Callback to be called on every rendered page.
   *
   * It can be used to transform the current route's rendered HTML.
   *
   * To do so, you can transform the route's rendered HTML (passed in through the `renderedHTML` parameter), and return it.
   * The returned value will be used as the HTML of the route.
   */
  onPageRendered?: (route: string, renderedHTML: string, appCtx: ViteReactSSGContext<true>) => Promise<string | null | undefined> | string | null | undefined
  /**
   * A function that is run after generation is complete.
   * It receives the build output directory as a string.
   *
   * You can use this to add, edit, or delete files in the output
   directory that you don't want to manage in React.
   */
  onFinished?: (dir: string) => Promise<void> | void
  /**
   * The application's root container `id`.
   *
   * @default `root`
   */
  rootContainerId?: string
  /**
   * The size of the SSG processing queue.
   *
   * @default 20
   */
  concurrency?: number
}
```
See [src/types.ts](https://github.com/Daydreamer-riri/vite-react-ssg/blob/main/src/types.ts). for more options available.
### Custom Routes to Render
[](#custom-routes-to-render)
You can use the `includedRoutes` hook to include or exclude route paths to render, or even provide some completely custom ones.
```js
// vite.config.js
export default {
  plugins: [],
  ssgOptions: {
    includedRoutes(paths, routes) {
      // exclude all the route paths that contains 'foo'
      return paths.filter(i => !i.includes('foo'))
    },
  },
}
```
```js
// vite.config.js
export default {
  plugins: [],
  ssgOptions: {
    includedRoutes(paths, routes) {
      // use original route records
      return routes.flatMap(route => {
        return route.name === 'Blog'
          ? myBlogSlugs.map(slug => `/blog/${slug}`)
          : route.path
      })
    },
  },
}
```
```ts
export default defineConfig({
  server: {
    https: true,
  },
})
```
### React17 Support
[](#react17-support)
-   for react18, with flag `useLegacyRender: true`, it will use the legacy `render` and `hydrate` methods.
-   for react17, on top of above, you will need minor update to react and react-dom [example](https://github.com/jesse23/webpack-test-bed/blob/main/scripts/define-react-exports.js) to polyfill the mjs import and the `react-dom/client`.

Links: [{"title":"How to Implement SSR & SSG in a React 19 App Using Vite with react-router-dom(Without Plugins) | by Dharmveer Singh | Medium","url":"https://medium.com/@singhdharmu89/how-to-implement-ssr-ssg-in-a-react-19-app-using-vite-with-react-router-dom-without-plugins-1646adce99c1"},{"title":"Vite React SSG - Getting-Started","url":"https://vite-react-ssg.netlify.app/docs/getting-started"},{"title":"GitHub - Daydreamer-riri/vite-react-ssg: Static-site generation for React on Vite. · GitHub","url":"https://github.com/Daydreamer-riri/vite-react-ssg"},{"title":"How to SSG a Vite SPA - Peterbe.com","url":"https://www.peterbe.com/plog/ssg-vite-spa"},{"title":"vite-react-ssg - npm","url":"https://www.npmjs.com/package/vite-react-ssg"},{"title":"Why Vite + React is the best fit for React developers","url":"https://www.vintasoftware.com/blog/vite-react-ssg-ssr"},{"title":"React Server Components | React Router","url":"https://reactrouter.com/how-to/react-server-components"},{"title":"Server-Side Rendering (SSR) | Vite","url":"https://vite.dev/guide/ssr"},{"title":"Vite setup for single static page using SSG | by Yoonji | Medium","url":"https://medium.com/@navynj/vite-setup-for-single-static-page-using-ssg-d84360595af4"},{"title":"Build a React app from Scratch – React","url":"https://react.dev/learn/build-a-react-app-from-scratch"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about vite-react-ssg with single route, no router, and React 19 compatibility:

## Single Route Setup (No Router)

For SSG of a single page only (without react-router-dom), you should import vite-react-ssg/single-page instead. This is specifically designed for cases where you only have a single route and don't need a router.

## React 19 Compatibility

While the search results don't explicitly mention React 19 compatibility, there are some relevant findings:

- React Router v7 now has built-in SSG support, and if you are using React Router v7, it's recommended to use its official SSG capabilities for better official support and integration.

- vite-react-ssg will continue to maintain SSG functionality for React Router v6 users.

## React 19 Specific Considerations

One article mentioned implementing SSR and SSG without plugins, with proper React Router DOM integration to handle navigation in React 19 applications with Vite, though this was focused on multi-route setups.

For a single-page setup without routing in React 19, the recommended approach would be to use the `vite-react-ssg/single-page` import path. However, I recommend checking the official vite-react-ssg documentation or GitHub repository for the latest React 19 compatibility details, as the documentation may have been updated since these search results.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

 `./app/src/components/curves/SaturationCurve.tsx`

```text
1	import { useMemo } from "react";
2	import { getSaturation } from "../../lib/palette";
3	import { usePaletteStore } from "../../store/paletteStore";
4	import type { ColorEntry } from "../../types/palette";
5	import { W, H, PAD, PLOT_W, PLOT_H, toSvgX, toSvgY } from "./chartConstants";
6	
7	interface SaturationCurveProps {
8	  entries: ColorEntry[];
9	  showDots: boolean;
10	}
11	
12	export function SaturationCurve({ entries, showDots }: SaturationCurveProps) {
13	  const sMod = usePaletteStore((s) => s.sMod);
14	  const saturation = usePaletteStore((s) => s.saturation);
15	  const satMode = usePaletteStore((s) => s.satMode);
16	
17	  const { curvePath, yMin, yMax, effectiveDots, gridLines, gridTop } = useMemo(() => {
18	    // Sample the curve at every integer lightness
19	    const points: { x: number; y: number }[] = [];
20	    for (let l = 0; l <= 100; l++) {
21	      points.push({ x: l, y: getSaturation(l, sMod, satMode) });
22	    }
23	
24	    const allY = points.map((p) => p.y);
25	    const yMin = Math.min(...allY, 0);
26	    const rawMax = Math.max(...allY);
27	    // Snap to next 0.25 step so the chart fills the plot area cleanly
28	    const yMax = Math.ceil(rawMax / 0.25) * 0.25;
29	
30	    // Build SVG path for the multiplier curve
31	    const pathParts = points.map((p, i) => {
32	      const cmd = i === 0 ? "M" : "L";
33	      return `${cmd}${toSvgX(p.x).toFixed(2)},${toSvgY(p.y, yMin, yMax).toFixed(2)}`;
34	    });
35	
36	    // Dots for each palette entry showing effective saturation
37	    const effectiveDots = entries.map((e) => {
38	      const sK = getSaturation(e.lightness, sMod, satMode);
39	      const effective = saturation * sK;
40	      return {
41	        cx: toSvgX(e.lightness),
42	        cy: toSvgY(sK, yMin, yMax),
43	        effective: Math.round(effective * 100),
44	        lightness: e.lightness,
45	        hex: e.hex,
46	      };
47	    });
48	
49	    // Horizontal grid lines at round values, always including 1.0
50	    const step = yMax - yMin > 1.5 ? 0.5 : 0.25;
51	    const gridVals: number[] = [];
52	    const lo = Math.ceil(yMin / step) * step;
53	    for (let v = lo; v <= yMax; v += step) {
54	      gridVals.push(parseFloat(v.toFixed(2)));
55	    }
56	    if (!gridVals.includes(1)) gridVals.push(1);
57	    const gridLines = gridVals
58	      .sort((a, b) => a - b)
59	      .map((v) => ({
60	        y: toSvgY(v, yMin, yMax),
61	        label: v.toFixed(2),
62	      }));
63	
64	    const gridTop = gridLines.length > 0 ? Math.min(...gridLines.map((g) => g.y)) : PAD.top;
65	
66	    return { curvePath: pathParts.join(""), yMin, yMax, effectiveDots, gridLines, gridTop };
67	  }, [sMod, saturation, satMode, entries]);
68	
69	  // Vertical grid lines at lightness landmarks
70	  const xGridValues = [0, 25, 50, 75, 100];
71	
72	  return (
73	    <div>
74	      <svg
75	        viewBox={`0 0 ${W} ${H}`}
76	        className="w-full h-auto"
77	        role="img"
78	        aria-labelledby="sat-curve-title"
79	      >
80	        <title id="sat-curve-title">Saturation adjustment curve</title>
81	        {/* Grid lines - horizontal */}
82	        {gridLines.map((g) => (
83	          <g key={g.label}>
84	            <line
85	              x1={PAD.left}
86	              y1={g.y}
87	              x2={W - PAD.right}
88	              y2={g.y}
89	              stroke="var(--color-stroke-grid)"
90	              strokeWidth={1}
91	            />
92	            <text
93	              x={PAD.left - 8}
94	              y={g.y + 3}
95	              textAnchor="end"
96	              className="text-base fill-text-faint"
97	            >
98	              {g.label}
99	            </text>
100	          </g>
101	        ))}
102	
103	        {/* Grid lines - vertical */}
104	        {xGridValues.map((l) => (
105	          <g key={l}>
106	            <line
107	              x1={toSvgX(l)}
108	              y1={gridTop}
109	              x2={toSvgX(l)}
110	              y2={H - PAD.bottom}
111	              stroke="var(--color-stroke-grid)"
112	              strokeWidth={1}
113	            />
114	            <text
115	              x={toSvgX(l)}
116	              y={H - PAD.bottom + 16}
117	              textAnchor="middle"
118	              className="text-base fill-text-faint"
119	            >
120	              {l}
121	            </text>
122	          </g>
123	        ))}
124	
125	        {/* Axis labels */}
126	        <text
127	          x={PAD.left + PLOT_W / 2}
128	          y={H - 4}
129	          textAnchor="middle"
130	          className="text-2xs fill-text-faint font-sans"
131	        >
132	          Lightness
133	        </text>
134	        <text
135	          x={12}
136	          y={PAD.top + PLOT_H / 2}
137	          textAnchor="middle"
138	          className="text-2xs fill-text-faint font-sans"
139	          transform={`rotate(-90, 12, ${PAD.top + PLOT_H / 2})`}
140	        >
141	          Multiplier
142	        </text>
143	
144	        {/* 1.0 baseline */}
145	        <line
146	          x1={PAD.left}
147	          y1={toSvgY(1, yMin, yMax)}
148	          x2={W - PAD.right}
149	          y2={toSvgY(1, yMin, yMax)}
150	          stroke="var(--color-stroke-axis)"
151	          strokeWidth={1}
152	          strokeDasharray="4 3"
153	        />
154	
155	        {/* Saturation curve */}
156	        <path
157	          d={curvePath}
158	          fill="none"
159	          stroke="var(--color-stroke-primary)"
160	          strokeWidth={2}
161	          strokeLinecap="round"
162	          strokeLinejoin="round"
163	        />
164	
165	        {/* Palette entry dots */}
166	        {showDots && effectiveDots.map((d) => (
167	          <circle
168	            key={d.lightness}
169	            cx={d.cx}
170	            cy={d.cy}
171	            r={3}
172	            fill={d.hex}
173	            stroke="var(--color-stroke-secondary)"
174	            strokeWidth={1}
175	          />
176	        ))}
177	      </svg>
178	
179	    </div>
180	  );
181	}
182

1	import type { CSSProperties } from "react";
2	import * as Slider from "@radix-ui/react-slider";
3	
4	interface SliderBaseProps {
5	  label: string;
6	  value: number;
7	  min: number;
8	  max: number;
9	  step: number;
10	  onValueChange: (value: number) => void;

14	
15	export function SliderBase({
16	  label,
17	  value,
18	  min,
19	  max,
20	  step,
21	  onValueChange,
22	  trackStyle,
23	  formatValue,
24	}: SliderBaseProps) {

26	
27	  return (
28	    <div className="flex flex-col gap-1.5 flex-1 min-w-[140px] sm:min-w-[200px]">

35	        </span>

 `./app/src/hooks/useDynamicFavicon.ts`

```text
1	import { useEffect, useRef } from "react";
2	import { usePaletteStore } from "../store/paletteStore";
3	
4	const SIZE = 32;
5	
6	function buildFaviconSvg(hue: number): string {
7	  return `<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 ${SIZE} ${SIZE}">
8	  <defs>
9	    <linearGradient id="sat" x1="0" y1="0" x2="1" y2="0">
10	      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="white"/>
11	      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="hsl(${hue}, 100%, 50%)"/>
12	    </linearGradient>
13	    <linearGradient id="val" x1="0" y1="0" x2="0" y2="1">
14	      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="black" stop-opacity="0"/>
15	      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="black"/>
16	    </linearGradient>
17	    <clipPath id="clip"><rect width="${SIZE}" height="${SIZE}"/></clipPath>
18	    <filter id="shadow"><feDropShadow dx="0" dy="0" stdDeviation="1.5" flood-color="black" flood-opacity="0.5"/></filter>
19	  </defs>
20	  <g clip-path="url(#clip)">
21	    <rect width="${SIZE}" height="${SIZE}" fill="url(#sat)"/>
22	    <rect width="${SIZE}" height="${SIZE}" fill="url(#val)"/>
23	  </g>
24	  <rect width="${SIZE}" height="${SIZE}" fill="none" stroke="hsl(0,0%,20%)" stroke-width="1.5"/>
25	  <path d="M24 2 Q-2 16 24 30" fill="none" stroke="white" stroke-width="2.5" stroke-linecap="round" filter="url(#shadow)"/>
26	</svg>`;
27	}
28	
29	function renderSvgToPngHref(svg: string): Promise<string> {
30	  return new Promise((resolve) => {
31	    const img = new Image();
32	    const blob = new Blob([svg], { type: "image/svg+xml;charset=utf-8" });
33	    const url = URL.createObjectURL(blob);
34	
35	    img.onload = () => {
36	      const canvas = document.createElement("canvas");
37	      canvas.width = SIZE;
38	      canvas.height = SIZE;
39	      const ctx = canvas.getContext("2d")!;
40	      ctx.drawImage(img, 0, 0, SIZE, SIZE);
41	      URL.revokeObjectURL(url);
42	      resolve(canvas.toDataURL("image/png"));
43	    };
44	
45	    img.src = url;
46	  });
47	}
48	
49	function setFavicon(href: string) {
50	  // Remove all existing icon links so browsers don't prefer a static fallback
51	  document
52	    .querySelectorAll<HTMLLinkElement>('link[rel="icon"]')
53	    .forEach((el) => el.remove());
54	
55	  const link = document.createElement("link");
56	  link.rel = "icon";
57	  link.type = "image/png";
58	  link.href = href;
59	  document.head.appendChild(link);
60	}
61	
62	export function useDynamicFavicon() {
63	  const hue = usePaletteStore((s) => s.hue);
64	  const pending = useRef(0);
65	
66	  useEffect(() => {
67	    const id = ++pending.current;
68	    const svg = buildFaviconSvg(hue);
69	
70	    renderSvgToPngHref(svg).then((href) => {
71	      // Drop stale renders if hue changed while we were rasterizing
72	      if (id !== pending.current) return;
73	      setFavicon(href);
74	    });
75	  }, [hue]);
76	}
77	
[result-id: r7]

1	import { useMemo } from "react";
2	import { usePaletteStore } from "../store/paletteStore";
3	import { generateNeutrals } from "../lib/palette";
4	import type { ColorEntry } from "../types/palette";
5	
6	export function usePalette(): ColorEntry[] {
7	  const hue = usePaletteStore((s) => s.hue);
8	  const saturation = usePaletteStore((s) => s.saturation);
9	  const sMod = usePaletteStore((s) => s.sMod);
10	  const satMode = usePaletteStore((s) => s.satMode);
11	  const lMin = usePaletteStore((s) => s.lMin);
12	  const lMax = usePaletteStore((s) => s.lMax);
13	
14	  return useMemo(
15	    () => generateNeutrals({ hue, saturation, sMod, satMode, lMin, lMax }),
16	    [hue, saturation, sMod, satMode, lMin, lMax],
17	  );
18	}

Replace `import { createRoot } from \"react-dom/client\"` with `import { ViteReactSSG } from \"vite-react-ssg\"` (or whichever export the library uses).\n2. Replace the `createRoot(...).render(...)` call with a route-based export, e.g.:\n   ```tsx\n   export const createRoot = ViteReactSSG(\n     { routes: [{ path: \"/\", Component: App }] },\n     ({ app }) => {\n       // optional app setup\n     }\n   );\n   ```\n   The exact API depends on vite-react-ssg's version — check docs for the current export pattern.\n3. The `StrictMode` and `MotionConfig` wrappers need to be preserved — either inline in the route component or via a layout wrapper. The route `Component` could be a wrapper that adds both, or they could be placed in `App.tsx` directly. The task doesn't specify where to land them — needs a decision.\n\n**What's missing:** The task doesn't address where `MotionConfig` moves if the entry format changes. If routes are defined externally, there's no obvious place for app-level wrappers unless vite-react-ssg provides a global setup callback or `App` itself wraps them. This needs to be decided during implementation.\n\n---\n\n### TASK-0049 — Audit browser-only code\n\n#### Files in TASK-0049's list\n\n**`useDynamicFavicon.ts`** — HIGH RISK. Uses: `new Image()`, `URL.createObjectURL()`, `URL.revokeObjectURL()`, `document.createElement(\"canvas\")`, `canvas.getContext(\"2d\")`, `canvas.toDataURL()`, `document.querySelectorAll()`, `document.createElement(\"link\")`, `document.head.appendChild()`. All of this runs inside `useEffect`, which does NOT run during SSR/SSG. The hook itself is safe from a rendering crash standpoint because it's effect-only. However, the functions `renderSvgToPngHref` and `setFavicon` reference browser globals at the module level (they are defined as standalone functions but only called from within the effect). They will not throw at import time. **Safe for SSG as-is** — no guard needed, but worth noting that favicon will simply not update until hydration.\n\n**`useCopyToClipboard.ts`** — Uses `navigator.clipboard.writeText()`. This is only called from the `copy` callback, never at module init or render time. **Safe for SSG as-is** — no guard needed.\n\n**`export.ts` (`downloadPalette`)** — Uses `URL.createObjectURL()`, `document.createElement(\"a\")`, `a.click()`. These are inside `downloadPalette()`, which is only called from a user interaction handler in `ExportDialog`. **Safe for SSG as-is** — no guard needed.\n\n**`TargetSwatch.tsx`** — Uses `canvas.getContext(\"2d\")`, `canvas.getBoundingClientRect()`, `document.addEventListener(\"mousemove\")`, `document.addEventListener(\"mouseup\")`. The canvas operations are called from `drawPicker()` which is called via a `useCallback` ref callback (`handleCanvasRef`) — only fires when the canvas DOM node mounts, never during SSR. The `document.addEventListener` calls are inside `useEffect`. **Safe for SSG as-is** — no guards needed.\n\n#### Additional files scanned\n\nI scanned every `.ts` and `.tsx` file in `app/src/` for: `document.`, `window.`, `navigator.`, `new Image`, `URL.createObjectURL`, `localStorage`, `sessionStorage`, `matchMedia`, `requestAnimationFrame`, `IntersectionObserver`, `ResizeObserver`, `MutationObserver`, `canvas.`, `.getContext`.\n\n**Only hits were in the four files already listed.** No additional files have browser-only API usage.\n\n**`main.tsx`** — `document.getElementById(\"root\")` is in the current entry point, but this will be entirely replaced by TASK-0048. Not a concern post-migration.\n\n**`paletteStore.ts`** — Pure Zustand store with no browser API calls. Safe.\n\n**SVG chart components (SaturationCurve.tsx, EffectiveSaturation.tsx)** — Pure SVG rendered via React. SSG-safe. Will render real HTML content.\n\n**Framer Motion components (CopyFeedback.tsx, ExportDialog.tsx, About.tsx)** — All use `AnimatePresence` and `motion.*` elements. Framer Motion v12 (which is what's installed) has SSR support. The `MotionConfig reducedMotion=\"user\"` in main.tsx correctly uses `\"user\"` not `\"always\"` — this is safe for SSR because it reads `prefers-reduced-motion` on the client only. No SSR-breaking patterns detected.\n\n**TASK-0049 file list assessment:** The list is complete — no missing files. However, the task's framing implies guards are needed. In practice, all four listed files are already safe because their browser API calls are gated behind effects or event handlers, not render paths. The task should be reframed: verify these files are safe rather than add guards. No code changes required for these files.\n\n---\n\n### TASK-0050 — Validate build output\n\n**File:** `app/index.html`\n\nCurrent `<body>`:\n```html\n<body>\n  <div id=\"root\"></div>\n  <script type=\"module\" src=\"/src/main.tsx\"></script>\n</body>\n```\n\n**What's accurate:** The task correctly identifies that `<div id=\"root\"></div>` is the empty shell that SSG should replace with pre-rendered HTML.\n\n**What the task doesn't address:** vite-react-ssg typically injects pre-rendered content directly into the `#root` div at build time. The current `index.html` is a standard Vite SPA shell — it won't need manual changes, the SSG build step handles it. The task should clarify *how* to validate: inspect `dist/index.html` post-build and confirm the `#root` div contains rendered markup rather than being empty.\n\n**Risk:** The palette grid and chart components reference state from Zustand. At SSG build time, the store will use its initial values (`hue: 220, saturation: 0.5`, etc.), so the pre-rendered HTML will show a valid default palette — good for crawlers.\n\n---\n\n### Missing Tasks\n\n**MT-1: Update package.json scripts for vite-react-ssg.**\n`build` and `preview` scripts need updating. Not covered by any of 0047–0050. This is required for CI and `npm run build` to actually invoke SSG.\n\n**MT-2: Verify Radix UI portals during SSG.**\n`ExportDialog` uses `Dialog.Portal` with `forceMount` and `TargetSwatch` uses `Popover.Portal`. Radix portals render into `document.body` — they will be absent from the pre-rendered HTML (portals render client-side only). This is expected behavior and not a bug, but the team should confirm this is acceptable. The dialog/popover content is not meaningful for crawlers anyway.\n\n**MT-3: TypeScript configuration check.**\n`vite-react-ssg` may require `\"moduleResolution\": \"bundler\"` or specific lib settings in `tsconfig.json`. The current `tsc -b` in the build script will run type checking before SSG build — if vite-react-ssg's types conflict with existing tsconfig settings, `tsc -b` will fail. Should be verified after install.\n\n---\n\n### Risk Areas\n\n**R1: Zustand store hydration mismatch.** Zustand initializes fresh on every SSR pass with default values. After hydration, if the client store immediately matches the server-rendered output (it will, since both start from the same defaults), there's no mismatch. Low risk, but worth watching in browser console for React hydration warnings.\n\n**R2: Framer Motion + SSG hydration.** `AnimatePresence` and `motion.*` components with `initial` animations may produce a hydration mismatch if Framer Motion applies `initial` state as inline styles during SSR but the client then animates from that state. Framer Motion v12 handles this but it's the most likely source of `console.error` hydration warnings. If warnings appear, adding `initial={false}` to `AnimatePresence` suppresses the enter animation on first render.\n\n**R3: `useDynamicFavicon` flash.** The dynamic favicon only applies after hydration. On first load, the static favicons from `index.html` will show until the effect runs. This is cosmetically fine but worth noting.\n\n**R4: Canvas in TargetSwatch inside Popover.** The canvas is rendered inside a `Popover.Content` which is portal-mounted — it won't exist in SSR output at all. The canvas `ref` callback (`handleCanvasRef`) calls `drawPicker` immediately on mount. Since mount only happens client-side (after portal injection), this is safe. No SSR crash risk.\n\n**R5: `vite: ^7.0.0` compatibility.** The project uses Vite 7. `vite-react-ssg` compatibility with Vite 7 should be verified before install — check the library's peer dependency requirements. As of mid-2025, most SSG libraries were still on Vite 5/6 peer deps. This could require a pinned or forked version.\n\n**R6: `@tailwindcss/vite` plugin order.** After swapping the React plugin for vite-react-ssg's equivalent, the Tailwind plugin must remain. Plugin order (`[ssgPlugin(), tailwindcss()]`) should be confirmed per vite-react-ssg docs to avoid CSS extraction issues.\n\n---\n\n### Summary\n\nTasks 0047–0050 are directionally correct but have these gaps:\n- **0047** is missing: remove `@vitejs/plugin-react`, update `build` and `preview` scripts (scripts update is entirely absent from all tasks).\n- **0048** is missing: where `StrictMode`/`MotionConfig` land in the new entry format needs explicit guidance.\n- **0049** is accurate on file list, but framing is wrong — none of the listed files need guards; they're already safe.\n- **0050** should add specific validation instructions (what to look for in `dist/index.html`).\n\nBiggest untracked risks: Vite 7 compatibility with vite-react-ssg (R5) and the missing scripts update (MT-1)."}}
```

The npm install instruction says `npm i -D vite-react-ssg react-router-dom` but `react-router-dom` is only needed for multi-route apps.\n\n2. **Browser API mocking**: By default `mock: false`. If SSG build fails due to `window`/`document` references during render (not in useEffect), set `ssgOptions.mock: true` in vite.config.ts. Optional but recommended for crawler performance.\n\n6. **The library is a CLI**, not a plugin. It wraps Vite's build process.
